How do these names reflect the complex power dynamics of the time, and what do they reveal about the role of education in shaping national identities? Similarly, what can we learn from the naming conventions of indigenous institutions, such as the University of Waikato in New Zealand or the First Nations University of Canada? By exploring the historical context behind these names, I hope to shed light on the ways in which education has been used as a tool of cultural imperialism, and how institutions are working to reclaim and redefine their identities in the present day.
